Please describe how patients and the public have been involved in developing this proposal.
Routes for recruitment have been added: parent support groups/networks, particularly working with national
parent carer networks to reach diverse communities and local organisations already working with CYP with
SEND, schools, social media and snowballing. It will be important to include parents/carers who have
experience of therapies and those who have not been able to access support to reflect diverse lived
experiences. We have included ‘trusted advocates’ to support recruitment and data collection with diverse
communities in recognition that trust and stigma around SEND are likely to be barriers to participation for
some communities and CYP. We will use interpreters and translation for workshop materials as needed
(heritage languages and/or BSL). Vouchers have been included as incentives for parents/carers and CYP.
Parents/carers and CYP with SEND have been added as an important participant group to be included in
WP2. A range of approaches, including workshops and written contributions with easy read information,
verbal explanations and interpreters (including BSL) were recommended to en

x and
Midlands (including Victoria Riley, Dave Harford), and regional Parent Carer Forum directors for Essex and
Midlands (Jo Harrison, Elaine Lambe). Parent Carer Forums will recruit 2 further parents/carers from diverse
communities in these regions. Separate groups of 4 CYP with SEND will be recruited in each region and
supported via local groups skilled in enabling participation e.g. Essex Multi-schools Academy. Our PPI work
has highlighted over-recruitment will be required.
PPI groups will meet three times per year and work closely with our public co-applicant and EP to oversee
and inform each WP:
WP1: Support development of review questions, protocols, interpretation of findings, final synthesis and
report.
WP2: Co-produce recruitment pathways for parents/carers and CYP and accessible recruitment
videos/visuals. Group members who work with community groups will act as ‘trusted advocates’ to reach
wider communities. Co-produce survey questions (WP2.1), focus group topic guides (WP2.2, 2.4), and
workshop design and materials (WP2.3) to ensure acceptability. Members from Parent Carer Forums will
facilitate workshops. PPI groups will support data analysis and development of Theory

of Change.
WP3: Inform contextual factors, collection of service provision data and contextual scenarios, and review
and validate findings.
Dissemination: Inform key audiences and effective ways of engaging these in dissemination activities, co-
produce easily accessible research summaries and a public-facing film/podcast and support dissemination
to managers and commissioners.
The GRIPP2 checklist will be used to plan and monitor PPI.
All members will be renumerated in accordance with NIHR guidance, taking a flexible approach to account
for individual circumstances and ensuring that there are no negative financial impacts of participation.
Training and support: The PPI groups will receive an induction, training and support from EP, SH and the
research team. Training and support will be provided by Parent Carer Forums on public involvement.
Separate meetings will be offered with EP before/after PPI group meetings to ensure members have been
able to communicate their views. CYP and parents will be supported to participate in meetings, using
communication supports as needed, by EP (a specialist SLT).

criteria.name: Inclusive Research Design
criteria.definition: Consideration of inclusivity throughout the research lifecycle, including participant diversity and accessibility.

signals.signal_text: Explicit strategies for diverse recruitment
Adaptive methods or accessibility considerations
Equity and participation indicators

evidence.text:
Please describe how patients and the public have been involved in developing this proposal.
Routes for recruitment have been added: parent support groups/networks, particularly working with national
parent carer networks to reach diverse communities and local organisations already working with CYP with
SEND, schools, social media and snowballing. It will be important to include parents/carers who have
experience of therapies and those who have not been able to access support to reflect diverse lived
experiences. We have included ‘trusted advocates’ to support recruitment and data collection with diverse
communities in recognition that trust and stigma around SEND are likely to be barriers to participation for
some communities and CYP. We will use interpreters and translation for workshop materials as needed
(heritage languages and/or BSL). Vouchers have been included as incentives for parents/carers and CYP.
Parents/carers and CYP with SEND have been added as an important participant group to be included in
WP2. A range of approaches, including workshops and written contributions with easy read information,
verbal explanations and interpreters (including BSL) were recommended to en

sure inclusivity of families
with different experiences who are experiencing difficult journeys, some will have
disabilities/communication needs. The involvement of organisations experienced in hosting public
involvement work has been added to support engagement with workshops (WP2), and organisations skilled
in supporting the participation of CYP in each region to support our PPI work.
Gathering information on communication supports and reasonable adjustments needed prior to workshops
and asking CYP if they would like parents/carers or a trusted adult to be included have all been added.
It was felt important that parents/carers and CYP participating in the WPs had very recent or current
experience of therapies/trying to access therapies.
The PPI groups will be involved throughout in supporting the design, conduct and dissemination of the
research. The use of accessible language for all public-facing documents was highlighted as critical.
Please describe the ways in which patients and the public will be actively involved in the proposed research,
including any training and support provided.
PPI groups will include 6 parents/carers of CYP with SEND including parents/carers from Esse

x and
Midlands (including Victoria Riley, Dave Harford), and regional Parent Carer Forum directors for Essex and
Midlands (Jo Harrison, Elaine Lambe). Parent Carer Forums will recruit 2 further parents/carers from diverse
communities in these regions. Separate groups of 4 CYP with SEND will be recruited in each region and
supported via local groups skilled in enabling participation e.g. Essex Multi-schools Academy. Our PPI work
has highlighted over-recruitment will be required.
PPI groups will meet three times per year and work closely with our public co-applicant and EP to oversee
and inform each WP:
WP1: Support development of review questions, protocols, interpretation of findings, final synthesis and
report.
WP2: Co-produce recruitment pathways for parents/carers and CYP and accessible recruitment
videos/visuals. Group members who work with community groups will act as ‘trusted advocates’ to reach
wider communities. Co-produce survey questions (WP2.1), focus group topic guides (WP2.2, 2.4), and
workshop design and materials (WP2.3) to ensure acceptability. Members from Parent Carer Forums will
facilitate workshops. PPI groups will support data analysis and development of Theory

evidence.text:
Please describe how patients and the public have been involved in developing this proposal.
Routes for recruitment have been added: parent support groups/networks, particularly working with national
parent carer networks to reach diverse communities and local organisations already working with CYP with
SEND, schools, social media and snowballing. It will be important to include parents/carers who have
experience of therapies and those who have not been able to access support to reflect diverse lived
experiences. We have included ‘trusted advocates’ to support recruitment and data collection with diverse
communities in recognition that trust and stigma around SEND are likely to be barriers to participation for
some communities and CYP. We will use interpreters and translation for workshop materials as needed
(heritage languages and/or BSL). Vouchers have been included as incentives for parents/carers and CYP.
Parents/carers and CYP with SEND have been added as an important participant group to be included in
WP2. A range of approaches, including workshops and written contributions with easy read information,
verbal explanations and interpreters (including BSL) were recommended to en

with different experiences who are experiencing difficult journeys, some will have
disabilities/communication needs. The involvement of organisations experienced in hosting public
involvement work has been added to support engagement with workshops (WP2), and organisations skilled
in supporting the participation of CYP in each region to support our PPI work.
Gathering information on communication supports and reasonable adjustments needed prior to workshops
and asking CYP if they would like parents/carers or a trusted adult to be included have all been added.
